entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: An important dryland cereal grown for food, feed, fodder, bioenergy and fiber

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country where sorghum is grown

entities.name: SWEET SORGHUM
entities.description: A new generation bioenergy crop characterized by high stalk yields and sugar accumulation

entities.name: GRAIN SORGHUM
entities.description: A type of sorghum used for grain production

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: The height of the plant, typically 3.0-4.0m for sweet sorghum

entities.name: MATURITY
entities.description: The time taken for the crop to reach maturity, noted as late maturing for sweet sorghum

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD SENSITIVITY
entities.description: The response of the plant to the length of day/night

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: STALK YIELD
entities.description: The amount of stalk biomass produced, comprising 70-75% of fresh biomass in sweet sorghum

entities.name: SUGAR CONTENT
entities.description: The percentage of sugars accumulated in the stalk, typically 10-15% for sweet sorghum

entities.name: SUGARCANE
entities.description: A crop used for sugar production, used as a comparison for sweet sorghum's sugar content and duration

entities.name: BAGASSE
entities.description: The leftover stalk after extraction of juice from sweet sorghum

entities.name: ANIMAL FEED
entities.description: Material used to feed livestock, which bagasse can be used for

entities.name: VERMI-COMPOSTING
entities.description: A process of composting using various worms, which bagasse can be used for

entities.name: POWER GENERATION
entities.description: The process of generating electricity, which bagasse can be used for

entities.name: AGROCLIMATIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions including climate and soil that affect crop growth

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall, to which sweet sorghum is tolerant

entities.name: SALINE-ALKALINE CONDITIONS
entities.description: Soil conditions characterized by high salt and alkalinity, to which sweet sorghum is tolerant

entities.name: CROP DURATION
entities.description: The time from planting to harvest, noted as 115-120 days for sweet sorghum

entities.name: MULTIPLE CROPPING SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural systems where two or more crops are grown on the same land in a year

entities.name: WATER USE
entities.description: The amount of water used by the crop, measured as 508 mm for sorghum

entities.name: SEASONAL EVAPOTRANSPIRATION
entities.description: The sum of evaporation and plant transpiration over a season
